title: Wild Rift Patch Notes 4.4
paragraphs: BOO! This patch is full of surprises, whether you’re looking for a fright or a Powerspike delight, we’ve got you covered!

We’re unleashing Fiddlesticks on the Rift, so watch your back, he’ll sneak up on you and give you and the champion you’re playing a good scare when you least expect it. We’re also celebrating our third-year anniversary this patch! Thank you for playing with us these past few years, you’ve made the Rift what it is, and we’re so excited to celebrate with you all. We’ve been buzzing about the goodies we’ll be giving out this year, so make sure to log in every day so you earn them all!
The update to mages is here, as well as a bunch of updates to items and some other systems across the Rift. We’ve also got a new Wild Pass, Ranked Season, and much more! Welcome to Patch 4.4!
We still have more in store for the other updates coming in 4.4, check out what else is to come in our latest Patch Preview:
Reminder: content will be released throughout the patch.

Something has awoken in Runeterra. Something ancient. Something terrible. The ageless horror known as Fiddlesticks stalks the edges of mortal society, drawn to areas thick with paranoia where it feeds upon terrorized victims. Wielding a jagged scythe, the haggard, makeshift creature reaps fear itself, shattering the minds of those unlucky enough to survive in its wake. Beware the sounding of the crow, or the whispering of the shape that appears almost human… Fiddlesticks has returned.
Fiddlesticks will be released on October 25 at 00:01 UTC.

Those Dream Raiders are still on our tail…thank god our private eye is finally in town, we needed the help! Psychic Detective Ekko is now available and will be your reward for completing this Wild Pass! You can also earn his Ascended version and an array of other goodies throughout the pass and in the Wild Pass Emporium! This Wild Pass Emporium will also include Dream Raider Nasus, Superhero Jayce, and Supervillain Jhin!
The Wild Pass will be available on October 25 at 00:01 UTC.

We’ve got an ash-ingment for you: climb the ranks and prove your skill in Ranked Season 11! As you play games, you’ll be able to earn this season's new skin Glorious Armada Ashe, and as always, her upgraded version will be available in the Ranked Store along with Glorious Crimson Nami!
Ranked S11 will be available on October 27 at 08:00 AM CST.

Later in Patch 4.4, you’ll be able to queue up solo or with your favorite duo in a brand-new mode! Arena is a 2v2v2v2 mode, where each player will be placed in teams of two where they will then duke it out in a series of duels in different arenas each map. Every round won will decrease the enemies’ health total, and the last team standing will be the winner! There will be different augments that players can select, making each game fun and unique!
Arena will be available on November 10 at 00:01 UTC.
Additionally, we’ve added some easter eggs for you that can trigger in some modes, so keep an eye out and let us know what surprises you encounter!
Complete missions and earn bubbly rewards!
The Coca-Cola Play As One Event begins October 25 at 00:01 UTC.
It’s not a party without some presents! Thanks for playing with us for these past three years, we’re so happy to celebrate you! Happy Third Year Anniversary!
The Anniversary Event begins October 25 at 00:01 UTC.

We’ve noticed that mages have not been performing well, and this patch we wanted to focus on showing them some love and helping them shine. Our main goals with our adjustments were to give them more survivability and mobility, so we’ve given a handful of mages extra movement speed, as well as some adjustments to wind up time on casting abilities and mobile cast. These changes should give some mages more opportunities to kite their enemies, especially in the later stages of the game.

We also wanted to give mages some more build options, since right now they’re lacking items to help them against heavily shielded foes, or in early-game skirmishes. A lot of builds are also tied to always having Rabadon’s Deathcap and Void Staff. One of the items we’re going to be adding to the shopkeeper's inventory is a classic, Mejai’s Soulstealer. If you’re feeling risky, you can pick it up and snowball your advantage if you get some early kills. Mages have also often struggled with pushing and clearing waves, because of the smaller map in Wild Rift, so we’ve also adjusted the positions of the bushes on both sides of the Mid Lane, which should give you more space to duke it out in 1v1s versus your lane opponent.
● Dealing physical damage to enemy champions applies 50% Grievous Wounds.

In previous patches we’ve seen that some items are too expensive to build. Even if you’re farming well on your first back, you aren’t able to pick up a full item, which delays your build and power spike. So, our goal with these adjustments is to give you a smoother build path. Additionally, we wanted to take a look at some of the marksmen items. While we’re happy with the changes we made to marksmen a few patches back, we wanted to make a few more adjustments to make some of the items more viable while also combining some of the effects from different items.

We’ve seen some incidents where the bleeding effect from Cauterize on monsters and minions would interrupt players' recalls, causing their back timers to be ruined or getting them caught by foes. So, we’ve removed the effect of Cauterize on monsters and minions so players can get their recall off as soon as possible.

We’ve noticed that the shield decay wasn’t as beneficial to players as we wanted it to be, so we’ve adjusted the shield decay mechanic and now the decay is based on the current shield value, rather than the maximum value.

Last patch we launched the new rune system, which added lots of new runes to grant your champ more ways to deal damage to enemies. To make it easier to see where your damage may be coming from or the value your runes are giving you in-game, we’ve added a new feature for rune effect stats. During game, you’ll now be able to view the benefits each Rune you’ve taken has given you in real time through the rune tab in the Strategy panel. And don’t worry if you forgot to check in-game or were too busy carrying to have a chance to check it out, you’ll also be able to view these stats after your game, as well as the stats from the runes your teammates and opponents that game took.

In previous patches, there were some issues with damage from champions to structures. The first was that champions with hybrid damage could not deal damage with part of their Attack Damage or Ability Power to turrets. The second issue was from champions with Ability Power items dealing damage to structures. The amount of damage they could deal, especially in the later stages of the game, was much higher than Attack Damage champs. So, we’ve made adjustments to address these problems.
It can be frustrating when the enemy team summons Rift Herald to attack the Nexus directly without minions. This also isn’t healthy for the pace of the game, so we’ve made some adjustments to Shelly.
Releasing October 25 @ 00:01 UTC
